name: Security Service Federal Credit Union
description_short: Security Service Federal Credit Union is a federally insured credit union offering checking, savings, mortgages, and investment services with nationwide ATM access and shared branching.
description_long:
Security Service Federal Credit Union is a federally insured credit union serving members with a commitment to improving their financial lives through accessible banking solutions. The organization operates multiple branches across Texas, Colorado, and Utah, with the Southeast Military location in San Antonio providing comprehensive in-person and digital banking services. The credit union emphasizes a service-oriented philosophy focused on understanding individual member needs and matching them with appropriate financial products. As a federal credit union, Security Service is member-owned and regulated by the NCUA, distinguishing it from traditional banks through its cooperative structure and member-focused mission.

Security Service offers a broad range of financial products and services including online banking, benefit-loaded checking accounts, high-interest savings deposits, mortgages, investment services, insurance agency services, credit cards with cash back rewards, and low-interest loans. Members can access their accounts through multiple channels: in-person at branch locations, online banking platforms, and an extensive network of ATMs. The Southeast Military branch provides specialized services including mortgage lending, investment advisory, and insurance products alongside standard banking transactions. Drive-through services extend accessibility with extended hours (8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m. weekdays), and additional conveniences include safe deposit boxes, night drops, and coin counters.

Security Service distinguishes itself through its nationwide accessibility via shared branching at thousands of credit union locations and free ATM access through three networks: approximately 30,000 CO-OP ATMs, over 600 Stripes locations across the Southwest, and Security Service's own ATM network. The credit union's member-owned structure allows it to reinvest profits into better rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks. The company emphasizes personalized service, taking time to understand individual member needs rather than offering one-size-fits-all solutions. The routing number 314088637 is specific to Security Service, and NCUA federal insurance protects member deposits.

Security Service is a legitimate, well-established financial institution with NCUA backing and broad product offerings. However, potential members should note that membership eligibility is required—not all consumers automatically qualify. The website does not provide specific APR rates, loan terms, or deposit rates, requiring prospective members to contact branches directly or visit the website for current rate information. As a credit union, membership benefits may be superior to traditional banking, but eligibility restrictions and the requirement to join as a member differ from universal-access banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline. For those with damaged credit, credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus. Secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to rebuilding credit scores over time.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Checking Account
features: No monthly maintenance fee (with qualifying deposit)

pros: NCUA federal insurance protects member deposits and ensures regulatory compliance
Nationwide shared branching network allows free basic transactions at thousands of credit union locations
Free ATM access through three networks: ~30,000 CO-OP ATMs, 600+ Stripes locations, and Security Service's own network
Extended drive-through hours (8 a.m. - 6 p.m. weekdays) for member convenience
Comprehensive services including mortgages, investment services, and insurance agency at branches
Member-owned credit union structure allows reinvestment of profits into competitive rates
Online banking platform available for digital access alongside in-person branch services
cons: Membership eligibility required—not all consumers automatically qualify to join
Website does not display specific APR rates, loan terms, or current deposit rates, requiring direct contact
Limited branch locations compared to major national banks (branches in TX, CO, UT only)
Saturday hours are limited (9 a.m. - 2 p.m.) with Sunday closure
No specific information about minimum balance requirements, fees, or early withdrawal penalties on deposits
best_for: Military-affiliated individuals eligible for Security Service membership seeking personalized service and competitive rates
Consumers wanting credit union benefits like member ownership, shared branching, and extensive ATM networks
Members seeking bundled services (checking, savings, mortgages, investments, insurance) from a single institution
Savers and borrowers in Texas, Colorado, or Utah looking for local branch support with nationwide access
diagnosis: Security Service Federal Credit Union is best for eligible members seeking a full-service financial institution with personalized service, competitive rates, and nationwide ATM/branching access through a member-owned cooperative structure. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is required and not universal—prospective customers must verify they qualify before applying, and specific current rates and terms must be obtained directly from the credit union since they are not displayed on this location page.
services: Online banking platform for account management and transactions
ATM access (drive-through deposit-accepting, branch, and nationwide networks)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
